sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first: a useful low-calorie flavor tool because as a swap for heavy sauces or dressings, it has some real value. can still earn points as a better swap because the category should reward harm reduction when it is real. use case can matter more than macros because this category is really about function and trade-offs.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: health claims are usually exaggerated because the item tends to be oversold way beyond what it deserves. rarely deserves to be treated as a foundation food because most of these are situational, not core nutrition plays. easy to overrate or misuse because this category attracts strong marketing and habits.

closing.summary: Apple Cider Vinegar does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best treated as an occasional context item, not a nutritional base.
closing.useCaseNote: Best treated as an occasional context item, not a nutritional base.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
